dataset: Natural Earth 10m Populated Places Simple + OpenStreetMap Hospitals (Overpass API) + Natural Earth 10m Roads
case_id: East African Community (Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi)

pi_items.source: Live environment test: file exists at this path, 56436 bytes, 163 Point features. Fields: name, population, country, latitude, longitude. Countries: Uganda=43, Tanzania=59, Kenya=36, Rwanda=11, Burundi=14. Population range 232 to 3,010,000. Derived from Natural Earth ne_10m_populated_places_simple.shp filtered by ogr2ogr WHERE adm0name IN ('Kenya','Tanzania','Uganda','Rwanda','Burundi'), with fields renamed: pop_max->population, adm0name->country.

Live environment test: file exists, 1,767,497 bytes, 6676 Point features from OSM Overpass API (amenity=hospital in bbox -12.0,28.5,5.0,42.0). Fields: name, type. Lon range 28.5562-41.8614, Lat range -11.9805-4.9491. Includes real named hospitals like 'M. P. Shah Hospital' (Nairobi), 'Kibosho Hospital' (Tanzania). If Overpass fails, deterministic fallback generates ~30-80 synthetic hospitals placed at communities sorted by name.

Live environment test: file exists, 662,241 bytes, 932 LineString features. Clipped from Natural Earth ne_10m_roads.shp to bbox 28.5,-12.0,42.0,5.0 via ogr2ogr -clipsrc. Fields: name, type, sov_a3. Road types: Unknown=492, Road=436, Major Highway=4. Many road segments have null name and sov_a3 fields.

Confirmed in task description, export_result.sh, and verifier.py. The exports directory exists at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/ with ga:ga ownership. File writing test passed (ga user can create and delete files there).
Confirmed in task description and export_result.sh. Expected columns: priority_class, community_count, total_population.

Confirmed: task description specifies these 7 fields. The input communities.geojson already has 'name', 'country', 'population' as native fields. The agent must compute nearest_facility_km and nearest_road_km via spatial joins, isolation_score via field calculator multiplication, and priority_class via CASE WHEN field calculator expression. Verifier checks for all 7 fields in the first feature's properties.

EPSG:32737 is WGS 84 / UTM zone 37S (https://epsg.io/32737). Central meridian 39E, covers 36E-42E in southern hemisphere. Verified available in environment via pyproj: Nairobi (36.8E, 1.3S) -> (255197, 9856204) in meters. NOTE: EAC bbox extends to 28.5E (western Rwanda/Burundi), which is outside the 36E-42E optimal zone. Distances for western communities will have some distortion but remain usable for this analysis (errors < 5% at 28.5E).

Live environment test: file exists (34,116 bytes), contains ground truth for 163 communities computed by setup_task.sh using pyproj EPSG:32737 projection, Euclidean point-to-point distances for hospitals, and point-to-line-segment distances for roads. Priority distribution: critical=3, high=5, moderate=36, low=119.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real geospatial data for the East African Community (Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi). The communities.geojson contains 163 Point features from Natural Earth 10m populated places with fields: name, population, country, latitude, longitude. Country distribution: Uganda 43, Tanzania 59, Kenya 36, Rwanda 11, Burundi 14. Population ranges from 232 to 3,010,000 (Nairobi). The health_facilities.geojson contains 6,676 hospital Points from OpenStreetMap Overpass API (amenity=hospital). The major_roads.geojson contains 932 LineString features from Natural Earth 10m roads. All data is in EPSG:4326 (WGS84). The agent must reproject to EPSG:32737 (UTM zone 37S) for metric distances, then use 'Join attributes by nearest' twice (once for hospitals, once for roads), then use Field Calculator to compute isolation_score = nearest_facility_km * nearest_road_km and a CASE WHEN expression for priority_class. Ground truth classification thresholds: critical = nearest_facility_km > 30 AND population > 10000; high = isolation_score > 50; moderate = isolation_score > 5; low = otherwise. Expected priority distribution: critical 3 communities (1.8%), high 5 (3.1%), moderate 36 (22.1%), low 119 (73.0%). The 3 critical communities are Karema (Tanzania, fac=59.44km, road=6.32km, pop=16,385), Kibiti (Tanzania, fac=72.24km, road=1.06km, pop=30,163), and Uvinza (Tanzania, fac=39.47km, road=0.57km, pop=80,350). The 5 high-priority communities are Kalangala (Uganda), Katwe (Uganda), Kipili (Tanzania), Konza (Kenya), and Korogwe (Tanzania). Output must include 2 files: community_accessibility.geojson with 7 fields (name, country, population, nearest_facility_km, nearest_road_km, isolation_score, priority_class) and priority_summary.csv with columns (priority_class, community_count, total_population). The QGIS Processing Toolbox has all required tools: 'Join attributes by nearest' (Vector general), 'Reproject layer' (Vector general), and 'Field calculator' (Vector table).
